task: Write a Python function title_words(s) returning the string with every whitespace-separated word title-cased (first letter upper, the rest lower) and runs of whitespace collapsed to single spaces, except that words already entirely uppercase stay exactly as they are. An empty string returns ''. Note one edge case it handles.
reason: The provided Python function correctly implements the required functionality, with well-structured code that meets the 'concise' requirement but could be improved in terms of readability. It handles an edge case where words already entirely uppercase are left unchanged.
trace: First, the function takes a string `s` as input and splits it into individual words. Then it iterates through each word. If the word is not entirely uppercase (i.e., not all characters are in uppercase), the first letter is converted to upper case and the rest of the letters are made lowercase. If the word is already entirely uppercase, it stays as it is. Finally, the function collapses multiple consecutive spaces into a single space using the `replace()` method and returns the resulting string. An edge case that this function handles is that words which are already title-cased (i.e., each word's first letter is uppercase and the rest is lowercase) remain as they are, unlike some other solutions that would change them to all uppercase.
